_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB/Equivalent (in CTVS, Pediatric Surgery, Thoracic Surgery)
clinical_indications: Presence of lung hydatid cyst of any size (surgery is gold standard treatment)
Cysts greater than 5 cm in diameter leading to bronchial compression
Symptoms such as cough, chest pain, breathlessness, expectoration, fever, hemoptysis, and anaphylactic phenomena associated with lung hydatid cyst

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did CT/MRI Chest report confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the histopathological examination report submitted?
Was the post-operative X-ray/CT Chest report submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Imaging was not indicative of surgery for hydatid cyst.
CT/MRI Chest report did not confirm the diagnosis of hydatid cyst.
Clinical notes were incomplete or missing, lacking detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure.
Histopathological examination report was not submitted or did not confirm the diagnosis of hydatid cyst.
Post-operative Chest X-ray or CT report was not submitted.

clinical_key_pointers: Hydatid disease involves the lungs (20-30%) by various mechanisms
The right lung is more commonly involved than the left
Cysts in the lungs are usually solitary and mostly unilateral
Lower lobe of the lungs is the most common site of pulmonary involvement, and there is a predilection for the posterior segments and the right lung
surgical_options: Cystostomy with closure of the bronchial openings and capitonnage (Posadas method)
Cystostomy with the closure of the bronchial openings alone
